steps.description: Click the "Technology" dropdown filter in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Python" option in the "Language" dropdown menu within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Python" checkbox option within the language dropdown in the Samples section of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button for the "Teams Conversation Bot using Python" sample in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code. And input ${{var:app_name:bot-conversation-python}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Run and Debug (Ctrl+Shift+D)" button in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT sidebar panel of Visual Studio Code, located beneath the "ACCOUNTS" section.
Click the green "Start Debugging" (F5) button in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug (Chrome)" option in the debug configuration dropdown menu within the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug" dropdown menu in the Run and Debug panel within Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Loading options" input field within the "Select a resource group" dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "+ New resource group" option in the "Select a resource group" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Type #text:${{var:app_name}}-${{var:random:###}}-rg into the "New resource group name" input field in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Press Enter to confirm the new resource group name "test-a12-rg" in the VS Code input dialog.
Click the "Central US" option in the location dropdown for a new resource group within Visual Studio Code, highlighting the recommended region.
Click the "Provision" button in the Azure account provisioning dialog at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
